Interface NumericExpression<N extends Number & Comparable<N>>

Type Parameters:
N - the type of the expression
All Superinterfaces:
ComparableExpression<N>, Expression<N>, Selection<N>, TupleElement<N>
public interface NumericExpression<N extends Number & Comparable<N>> extends ComparableExpression<N>
Type for number query expressions.
Since:
4.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    NumericExpression<N>
    abs()
    Create an expression that returns the absolute value of this expression.
    NumericExpression<Double>
    avg()
    Create an aggregate expression applying the avg operation.
    NumericExpression<N>
    ceiling()
    Create an expression that returns the ceiling of this expression, that is, the smallest integer greater than or equal to this expression.
    NumericExpression<N>
    coalesce(Expression<? extends N> y)
    Create an expression that returns null if this and the argument evaluate to null, and the value of the first non-null expression otherwise.
    NumericExpression<N>
    coalesce(N y)
    Create an expression that returns null if this and the argument evaluate to null, and the value of the first non-null expression otherwise.
    NumericExpression<N>
    dividedBy(Expression<? extends N> y)
    Create an expression that returns the quotient of this expression divided by the argument.
    NumericExpression<N>
    dividedBy(N y)
    Create an expression that returns the quotient of this expression divided by the argument.
    NumericExpression<N>
    dividedInto(N y)
    Create an expression that returns the quotient of this expression dividing the argument.
    NumericExpression<Double>
    exp()
    Create an expression that returns the exponential of this expression, that is, Euler's number e raised to the power of its argument.
    NumericExpression<N>
    floor()
    Create an expression that returns the floor of this expression, that is, the largest integer smaller than or equal to this expression.
    Predicate
    ge(Expression<? extends Number> y)
    Create a predicate for testing whether this expression is greater than or equal to the first argument.
    Predicate
    ge(Number y)
    Create a predicate for testing whether this expression is greater than or equal to the first argument.
    Predicate
    gt(Expression<? extends Number> y)
    Create a predicate for testing whether this expression is greater than the first argument.
    Predicate
    gt(Number y)
    Create a predicate for testing whether this expression is greater than the first argument.
    Predicate
    le(Expression<? extends Number> y)
    Create a predicate for testing whether this expression is less than or equal to the first argument.
    Predicate
    le(Number y)
    Create a predicate for testing whether this expression is less than or equal to the first argument.
    NumericExpression<Double>
    ln()
    Create an expression that returns the natural logarithm of this expression.
    Predicate
    lt(Expression<? extends Number> y)
    Create a predicate for testing whether this expression is less than the first argument.
    Predicate
    lt(Number y)
    Create a predicate for testing whether this expression is less than the first argument.
    NumericExpression<N>
    max()
    Create an aggregate expression applying the numerical max operation.
    NumericExpression<N>
    min()
    Create an aggregate expression applying the numerical min operation.
    NumericExpression<N>
    minus(Expression<? extends N> y)
    Create an expression that returns the difference between this expression subtracted by the argument.
    NumericExpression<N>
    minus(N y)
    Create an expression that returns the difference between this expression subtracted by the argument.
    NumericExpression<N>
    negated()
    Create an expression that returns the arithmetic negation of this expression.
    NumericExpression<N>
    nullif(Expression<? extends N> y)
    Create an expression that tests whether this expression is equal to the argument, returning null if they are and the value of the first expression if they are not.
    NumericExpression<N>
    nullif(N y)
    Create an expression that tests whether this expression is equal to the argument, returning null if they are and the value of the first expression if they are not.
    NumericExpression<N>
    plus(Expression<? extends N> y)
    Create an expression that returns the sum of this expression and its argument.
    NumericExpression<N>
    plus(N y)
    Create an expression that returns the sum of this expression and its argument.
    NumericExpression<Double>
    power(Expression<? extends Number> y)
    Create an expression that returns this expression raised to the power of its first argument.
    NumericExpression<Double>
    power(Number y)
    Create an expression that returns this number expression raised to the power of its first argument.
    NumericExpression<N>
    round(Integer n)
    Create an expression that returns this number expression rounded to the number of decimal places given by the first argument.
    NumericExpression<Integer>
    sign()
    Create an expression that returns the sign of this expression, that is, 1 if this expression is positive, -1 if this expression is negative, or 0 if this expression is exactly zero.
    NumericExpression<Double>
    sqrt()
    Create an expression that returns the square root of this expression.
    NumericExpression<N>
    subtractedFrom(N y)
    Create an expression that returns the difference between this expression subtracted from the argument.

    Create an aggregate expression applying the sum operation.

    Create an aggregate expression applying the sum operation, returning a Double result.

    Create an aggregate expression applying the sum operation, returning a Long result.

    Create an expression that returns the product of this expression multiplied by the argument.

  • Method Details

    • gt

      Predicate gt(Expression<? extends Number> y)
      Create a predicate for testing whether this expression is greater than the first argument.
      Parameters:
      y - expression
      Returns:
      greater-than predicate
      See Also:
      • CriteriaBuilder.gt(Expression, Expression)

    • gt

      Predicate gt(Number y)
      Create a predicate for testing whether this expression is greater than the first argument.
      Parameters:
      y - value
      Returns:
      greater-than predicate
      See Also:
      • CriteriaBuilder.gt(Expression, Number)

    • ge

      Predicate ge(Expression<? extends Number> y)
      Create a predicate for testing whether this expression is greater than or equal to the first argument.
      Parameters:
      y - expression
      Returns:
      greater-than-or-equal predicate
      See Also:
      • CriteriaBuilder.ge(Expression, Expression)

      Returns:
      sign

      Returns:
      the base raised to the power of the exponent

      Returns:
